steps.title: Check current jaeger deployment
steps.description:
Verify your jaeger version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n jaeger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
helm list -n jaeger 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working jaeger installation.

steps.title: Review jaeger configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant jaeger configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n jaeger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
kubectl get configmap -n jaeger -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=jaeger
```
## Requirement - what kind of business use case are you trying to solve?

Elastic recently released Elasticsearch 8.0, by now they've released 8.1.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Elasticsearch 8.x support
steps.description:
## Which problem is this PR solving?
- Resolves  #3571

## Short description of the changes
- Modifies the index mapping to use the ES 7 mappings for higher versions too.
- Modifies the tests to include ES 8
- Modifies the github ci pipeline to add ES 8.1.0
```yaml
The https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/8.1/migrating-8.0.html lists now (somewhere in the middle)
```

steps.title: Upgrade jaeger to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade jaeger:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade jaeger jaeger/jaeger --namespace jaeger
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n jaeger
helm list -n jaeger
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n jaeger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
kubectl get events -n jaeger --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Elasticsearch 8.x support" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Which problem is this PR solving?
- Resolves  #3571

## Short description of the changes
- Modifies the index mapping to use the ES 7 mappings for higher versions too.
- Modifies the tests to include ES 8
- Modifies the github ci pipeline to add ES 8.1.0
codeSnippets: The https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/8.1/migrating-8.0.html lists now (somewhere in the middle)

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with jaeger installed or the issue environment reproducible.
